ps140: You took your cross upon your shoulder and crucified yourself to the world,* and followed in the steps of God the Word* who created all things out of nothingness, as He said,* and shook off the love of the world and every passionate attachment.* Therefore, shining forth in the virtues, O father,* through divine works you received an eternal inheritance.

You were shown to be a mystic clarion rousing all to spiritual hymnody,* and a harp sounded by God, praising the regiments of God,* glorifying all the saints and proclaiming their victories,* for you drew forth an abyss of words from the springs of salvation,* giving drink to the house of God.

Crucified with love, you were shown to be a treasury of the virtues,* and, chanting sacred hymns, you sought out all* who were well-pleasing to God in their lives,* emulating with faith their divine corrections;* and sharing therein in godly manner, you were shown to be a partaker of sweetness.
1, Podoben “The joy of the heavenly ranks…”

As a beautiful vessel of dispassion containing the radiance of life, O all-blessed father George,* by abstinence you enslaved the passions to your thought;* and you were a destroyer of the demons* and a healer of sufferings, O right wondrous one.

Let George now be praised, who was the pure vial of the divine Spirit,* the well-spring of miracles, the pillar of monastics,* the meek emulator of the meek Christ,* the earthly angel and heavenly man.

Steering the ship of your soul toward the calm harbour,* you escaped the turmoil of life as from the waves of the sea,* and soared aloft to God, away from the earth,* and were shown to be like an angel while yet in the flesh.
theotokion: In that you are the help of helpless men,* the hope of all the hopeless, and a release from cruel circumstances, O Theotokos,* be my helper, O all-pure one, delivering me from sins and tribulations,* in that you are merciful.
stavrotheotokion: Standing before the Cross of your Son and God,* and witnessing His long-suffering, O pure Mother,* weeping, you said:* Woe is me, O my most sweet Child!* How is it that You endure these things unjustly inflicted upon You, O Word of God,* that You may save mankind?
